learnings.question: What psychological concept describes the phenomenon where writing down or digitally saving an idea signals the brain that it has permission to forget it?
options: The Performance Paradox of Cognitive Offloading
learnings.learning: Writing down or digitally saving information triggers a psychological phenomenon known as the 'Performance Paradox' of Cognitive Offloading, where the brain actively forgets the recorded information because it treats the external storage as permission to delete it.
learnings.articleTitle: Is Your Brain the Worst Place to Store Ideas? Why I Built a Second One

learnings.question: When analyzing the frequency of words in the Quran, approximately what percentage of the text do the top 20 most repeated words actually accumulate to?
learnings.learning: While the Pareto Principle (80/20 rule) suggests that 20% of effort yields 80% of results, word frequency analysis of the Quran reveals that its top 20 most repeated words actually account for only about 20.83% of the total text.
learnings.articleTitle: Top 20 Words to Understand the Quran: A Data Analysis using Python

learnings.question: What is the standard format of a User Story in a Scrum Product Backlog?
options: As a [role], I need [feature], so that [reason]
learnings.learning: In Scrum, a User Story within the Product Backlog follows a specific structured format: 'As a [role], I need [feature], so that [reason]'.

learnings.question: Which concept refers to the actual unfolding of events in a person's life that can be actively influenced or altered by their decisions and supplications?
learnings.learning: In Islamic theology, Qada' refers to the actual unfolding and occurrence of events in a person's life which can be altered through actions and supplication, whereas Qadr represents the complete, preordained plan eternally known to God.
learnings.articleTitle: The Art of Qada’ and Qadr

learnings.question: Within the PCIe power management framework, what is the primary trade-off when a device transitions to deeper power-saving states like L1.2 or L2?
options: Wake-up latency is completely eliminated by keeping the electrical circuits fully powered.
Greater energy savings are achieved at the cost of a longer wake-up time to resume activity.
Faster wake-up times are achieved at the cost of higher active power consumption.
Higher data transmission rates are maintained while shutting down the main link.
learnings.learning: PCIe power management utilizes a spectrum of states (from L0 to L2) that trade off power consumption for responsiveness, meaning deeper power-saving states yield maximum energy savings but require the longest wake-up latencies to resume activity.
learnings.articleTitle: Power Management States in the context of PCIe
